title: ACSM's Guidelines for Exercise Testing and Prescription (12th Edition)
content: The ACSM framework emphasizes individualized exercise prescription by combining baseline assessment, progression principles, and risk management. Programs should adjust frequency, intensity, time, and type according to goals and response.

title: NSCA Essentials of Strength Training and Conditioning (5th Edition)
content: Strength program design should be periodized and goal-specific. Progression can be achieved by manipulating intensity, volume, exercise selection, rest intervals, and weekly structure while tracking adaptation and fatigue.

title: Advanced Sports Nutrition (3rd Edition)
content: Sports nutrition planning links training demand with energy availability, carbohydrate periodization, protein distribution, hydration strategy, and recovery fueling based on athlete needs and schedule.

title: A systematic review, meta-analysis and meta-regression of protein supplementation on resistance training-induced gains in muscle mass and strength
content: Protein supplementation can improve lean mass outcomes during resistance training, especially when baseline intake is lower. Benefits plateau as daily protein intake approaches an evidence-based target range.

title: Dose-response relationship between weekly resistance training volume and increases in muscle mass
content: Higher weekly training volume is generally associated with greater hypertrophy, though practical limits and recovery constraints must be considered. Program progression should balance stimulus and recoverability.

title: International Society of Sports Nutrition Position Stand: protein and exercise
content: Position statements summarize evidence for daily protein targets, per-meal distribution, and timing around exercise. Recommendations vary with training status, energy availability, and performance goals.

title: Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans
authors: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
content: General adult guidance emphasizes regular aerobic activity and muscle-strengthening sessions each week. Dose can be scaled according to baseline status and health context.

title: WHO Guidelines on physical activity and sedentary behaviour
content: WHO guidance provides population-level recommendations for activity and sedentary behavior reduction across age groups, with emphasis on regular movement, progression, and inclusivity.

title: Nutrition and Physical Fitness (Open Textbook)
content: Open educational chapters connect core nutrition concepts with physical activity applications, including weight management, macro planning, and practical behavior strategies.
